2_open_and_merge_ops_pr.title: fix(travel-agent): render_amadeus_failure returns ok status so widget surfaces in prompt

4_smoke_terminal_all_intents.notes: All three intents completed at the NoETL/playbook level and retained render payloads in step data. The prompt history visibility assertion still failed: running the travel command from the terminal-style prompt produced a completed execution but no widget appeared inline.

5_smoke_canvas.notes: The deployed /travel canvas still displayed the old api_integration/amadeus_ai_api wording and POST /execute returned HTTP 422, so the flagship travel runtime was not exercised from the canvas surface in this round.

additional_prompt_auto_render_probe.diagnosis: The one-line status->outcome fix works: render_amadeus_failure no longer fails the execution. However, completed travel executions still expose no top-level execution.result.render. The GUI watcher/report path appears to depend on top-level result.render, so the widget remains invisible even though the step-level render payload exists.

regressions_found.name: completed travel executions have result=- for GUI watcher/report
regressions_found.details: The backend execution completes and the render widget exists in step data, but no top-level execution.result.render is persisted. The NoETL prompt auto-render watcher did not surface the widget after more than 60 seconds, and report showed result=-.

regressions_found.name: /travel canvas still targets old api_integration/amadeus_ai_api path
regressions_found.details: The deployed travel canvas displayed the old api_integration/amadeus_ai_api helper text and POST /execute returned 422, so the canvas widget smoke could not validate the new flagship travel runtime.

notes: The ops PR successfully fixed the NoETL step-failure convention problem: result.status='failed' was renamed to result.outcome='amadeus_failure'.
All three terminal intents completed at the NoETL level after re-registering travel runtime version 5.

The remaining gap is not the original step status bug. It is render propagation/visibility: the GUI has no top-level render payload to attach in prompt history, and /travel still appears to be wired to the old playbook surface.
No GREEN validation log paragraph was appended because the acceptance criterion required widgets visible in prompt/canvas, and that did not pass.
deferred_followups: Make the travel runtime's final/top-level execution result carry the selected render payload, or update the GUI watcher/report path to extract the latest render payload from persisted events/step variables.
Update and redeploy the /travel canvas to target automation/agents/travel/runtime instead of api_integration/amadeus_ai_api, then repeat the canvas smoke.
Add a playbook authoring guide note: do not use result.status='failed' for semantic failure states; use outcome, kind, category, or another non-magic field.
After noetl#88 preserves HTTP response bodies on error, consider returning Amadeus calls to kind:http with an error-envelope option instead of urllib wrappers.
Phase 2 travel follow-up remains: route travel agent API calls through mcp/amadeus internally and add hotels/activities branches.
Phase 3 travel follow-up remains: restore multi-provider parity with a keychain-compatible mechanism.
